title: Entadopsis polyphylla
intro: Entadopsis polyphylla, also known as the "Polyphylla Entada", is a medicinal plant species native to tropical regions, characterized by its climbing habit and large, flat pods. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Entadopsis polyphylla include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Entadopsis polyphylla is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tablets, often in combination with other medicinal plants, to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Entadopsis polyphylla is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of legume plants. The genus Entadopsis is further divided into several species, with E. polyphylla being one of them. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its leaf structure and flower arrangement. Within the Fabaceae family, Entadopsis polyphylla is placed in the subfamily Faboideae, which includes many other legume species. Further classification may be possible at the genus or species level, but these are the general categories in which Entadopsis polyphylla is placed.

It is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to its potential health benefits.
